data.content: The MSTest 3.6.3 doesn't have `ThrowsExactly` — I need to upgrade the packages. Let me also fix the implicit usings issue:

arguments.old_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.7.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.7.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is that `Assert.ThrowsExactly` is only available in MSTest v4+, and the project is using MSTest 3.6.3. Also, `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` needs `using System;`. Let me check the MSTest version issue. MSTest 3.6.3 has `Assert.ThrowsExactly` - wait, actually let me check. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` was introduced in MSTest 3.7 or maybe it's available in v3.6.3... Actually checking: `ThrowsExactly` was introduced in MSTest v4. In MSTest v3, the equivalent is `Assert.ThrowsException`.


Actually, I need to reconsider this. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` was introduced in MSTest 3.7.0, but the project is on 3.6.3, so it's not available. I'll need to either upgrade the MSTest package or use `Assert.ThrowsException` instead, which is available in the current version.

Since the user wants to modernize to current best practices with `ThrowsExactly`, I should upgrade the MSTest packages. I also notice the `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` error in `PriceCalculator.cs` — this might be a missing `using System;` statement or an issue with implicit usings not being enabled in the project file. The issue is that `Assert.ThrowsExactly` isn't available in MSTest 3.6.3—it was added in 3.7.0. I need to upgrade MSTest to a newer version like 3.8.0, and also enable implicit usings in the project file to resolve the System namespace errors.

data.content: All 5 tests pass. Let me show the final state of both files:

All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the modernizations applied:

| Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|
| `public class` | `public sealed class` |
| `TestContext?` nullable | `required TestContext { get; init; }` |
| `[TestInitialize]` + mutable field | `readonly` field with inline init |
| `[ExpectedException]` | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` |
| Swapped `Assert.AreEqual` args | Corrected to `(expected, actual)` |
| Hard cast + `Assert.IsTrue` | `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>()` + `StringAssert.Contains` |
| Manual `foreach` test cases | `[DataRow]` with `DisplayName` |
| Vague test names (`Test1`) | Descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` naming |
| Separate MSTest packages | Single `MSTest` v4.2.2 meta-package |
